notes: Nineteenth validated clue batch compiled from official school websites.
Clues intentionally avoid revealing enrollment size, grade span, or directional/proximity information because those are gameplay feedback channels.

clues.text: Its mission says the school exists to love and value scholars and communities by leading a movement of excellence in education.
Official history pages say the first Movement campus opened on Freedom Drive in 2017.
The school says it equips scholars with self-efficacy, access to world-class instruction, and financial education to close generational gaps.
Its wellness policy says the school is committed to environments that protect children's health and well-being.
Movement's North Carolina board page lists Garrett McNeill as chair.
Official leadership pages list Kerri-Ann T. Thomas as CEO.
Official pages say the campus is a community of the nations that celebrates the diversity of East Charlotte and the world.
The school highlights three distinctives: small-school setting, lead with virtue, and school of the nations.
Its principal message says the school grows the whole scholar socially, emotionally, and academically.
Official pages say the school emphasizes community circle and strong family partnerships.
Movement's North Carolina board page lists Garrett McNeill as chair.
Official leadership pages list Chelsea Hobbing as principal.
Official materials say the school opened in August 2023.
The school highlights small-school setting and leading with virtue as central distinctives.
Its principal message says learning comes first, always.
Official pages say the principal's leadership has contributed to measurable academic growth, including two North Carolina Academic Growth Awards.
Movement's North Carolina board page lists Garrett McNeill as chair.
Official leadership pages list Kenneth Gorham as principal.
Official materials say the school opened in August 2022.
The school describes itself as a brand new facility in Southwest Charlotte.
Its campus information highlights family engagement as a shared responsibility between school, parents, and community.
Official pages say the school uses parent engagement to strengthen academic, behavioral, and emotional success.
Movement's North Carolina board page lists Garrett McNeill as chair.
Official leadership pages list Dr. Shenaille Beckley Danso as principal.
Official materials say the school became TMSA Charlotte in June 2022 after Queen City STEM School joined the TMSA family.
Its public-facing motto is "Science, Wisdom, Peace."
The school says it focuses on STEAM and character education in a safe, nurturing, and culturally responsive learning environment.
Official pages say the TMSA model emphasizes whole-child focus alongside rigorous academics.
TMSA governance pages list Victor Ofori-Boadu as board president.
Official pages list Mr. Aydin as principal of the secondary campus and Amani Saeed as principal of the elementary campus.
